speaker: Antonio
quote: I hold the world but as the world, Gratiano, a stage where every man must play a part, and mine a sad one.

speaker: Bassanio
quote: Gratiano speaks an infinite deal of nothing, more than any man in all Venice.

speaker: Portia
quote: If to do were as easy as to know what were good to do, chapels had been churches.

speaker: Portia
quote: So is the will of a living daughter curbed by the will of a dead father.

speaker: Shylock
quote: I hate him for he is a Christian.

speaker: Antonio
quote: The devil can cite Scripture for his purpose!

speaker: Shylock
quote: A pound of your fair flesh, to be cut off and taken in what part of your body pleaseth me.

speaker: Bassanio
quote: I like not fair terms and a villain's mind.

speaker: Arragon
quote: How much unlike art thou to Portia! How much unlike my hopes and my deservings!

speaker: Arragon
quote: Who chooseth me shall have as much as he deserves.

speaker: Portia
quote: To offend, and judge, are distinct offices and of opposed natures.

speaker: Portia
quote: Thus hath the candle singed the moth.

speaker: Portia
quote: O, these deliberate fools! When they do choose, they have the wisdom by their wit to lose.

speaker: Nerissa
quote: The ancient saying is no heresy, hanging and wiving goes by destiny.

speaker: Arragon
quote: Did I deserve no more than a fool's head?

speaker: Shylock
quote: Hath not a Jew eyes? Hath not a Jew hands, organs, dimensions, senses, affections, passions?

speaker: Shylock
quote: If you prick us, do we not bleed?

speaker: Shylock
quote: The villainy you teach me I will execute, and it shall go hard but I will better the instruction.

speaker: Song
quote: Tell me where is fancy bred, or in the heart, or in the head?

speaker: Bassanio
quote: So may the outward shows be least themselves; the world is still deceived with ornament.

speaker: Scroll
quote: You that choose not by the view chance as fair and choose as true.

speaker: Duke
quote: A stony adversary, an inhuman wretch, uncapable of pity, void and empty from any dram of mercy.

speaker: Portia
quote: The quality of mercy is not strained. It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven.

speaker: Portia
quote: When mercy seasons justice.

speaker: Bassanio
quote: Do all men kill the things they do not love?

speaker: Bassanio
quote: The Jew shall have my flesh, blood, bones and all, ere thou shalt lose for me one drop of blood.

speaker: Antonio
quote: I am a tainted wether of the flock, meetest for death.

speaker: Shylock
quote: My deeds upon my head! I crave the law, the penalty and forfeit of my bond.

speaker: Portia
quote: This bond doth give thee here no jot of blood; the words expressly are 'a pound of flesh.'

speaker: Shylock
quote: You take my house when you do take the prop that doth sustain my house.

speaker: Lorenzo
quote: The moon shines bright: in such a night as this, when the sweet wind did gently kiss the trees.

speaker: Portia
quote: How far that little candle throws his beams! So shines a good deed in a naughty world.

speaker: Nerissa
quote: When the moon shone, we did not see the candle.

speaker: Portia
quote: Nothing is good, I see, without respect: methinks it sounds much sweeter than by day.

speaker: Portia
quote: This night methinks is but the daylight sick; it looks a little paler.

speaker: Portia
quote: Let me give light, but let me not be light; for a light wife doth make a heavy husband.

speaker: Bassanio
quote: By heaven, it is the same I gave the doctor!

speaker: Portia
quote: Even so void is your false heart of truth. By heaven, I will ne'er come in your bed until I see the ring.

speaker: Gratiano
quote: Why, this is like the mending of highways in summer, where the ways are fair enough.

speaker: Antonio
quote: Sweet lady, you have given me life and living; for here I read for certain that my ships are safely come to road.
